description_long: This fire hydrant is a robust, cylindrical structure made primarily of metal and painted in a uniform red color. It features a domed top with a central nut, a prominent central body, and a wide, octagonal base for stability. There are two large side outlets for hose connections, each capped, and a single front-facing outlet with a hexagonal operating nut or cap. The hydrant's surface appears matte with some subtle paneling and bolt details, giving it an industrial, utilitarian look. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible on the exterior. The overall design is typical of fire hydrants found in urban environments, intended for emergency water access.
description: A sturdy, red, cylindrical fire hydrant with a domed top, two side outlets, and a front-facing cap, all set on a wide octagonal base. The surface is matte and industrial, with no visible markings or chains.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet with a hexagonal cap and one side outlet extending to the right.
description_view_1: Side view displaying both side outlets extending horizontally, with the front cap partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with the back of the hydrant body and the left side outlet most visible, front cap not visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing both side outlets, similar to view 1 but from the other direction, with no front cap visible.
